behaviors.description: A cell containing `=SUM(A1:A10)` evaluates to the correct sum of values in A1..A10.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local formula semantics (range expansion + numeric aggregation) with no required module handoff beyond one evaluator path (MOCK-MISS: no). No OS/network/subprocess (REAL-INFRA: no), no AI output dependence (LLM-DEP: no), no multi-agent chain (MULTI-STEP: no). Rejected higher levels because behavior text is only arithmetic correctness for one formula and does not require persistence, recalculation graph, or external infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Call the formula evaluator on a deterministic in-memory sheet fixture with A1..A10 populated, assert computed SUM equals expected numeric total across normal and edge numeric values.

behaviors.description: A cell referencing another formula cell (e.g., `=B1+1`, B1 is `=A1*2`) produces the correct cascaded result.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at module handoff between reference resolver, dependency graph, and evaluator (MOCK-MISS: yes). Integration-brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required; (b) no AI/ML output; (c) no agentic chain; (d) not LLM orchestration. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is purely in-memory formula cascading.
behaviors.test_description: Construct an in-memory workbook with chained references across at least 2 levels, trigger evaluation, and assert final value plus intermediate dependency resolution order/state are correct.

behaviors.description: Circular dependency (A1↔B1) is detected and returns a circular-reference error instead of looping.
behaviors.justification: Bug typically emerges when graph construction and evaluator recursion/iteration interact (MOCK-MISS: yes). Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess, no AI output, no multi-agent sequence. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text only concerns dependency-cycle handling inside engine logic.
behaviors.test_description: Create workbook cells with direct and indirect cycles, run evaluation, assert explicit circular-reference error is returned and execution terminates without hang/timeouts.

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP exact match returns correct table value; missing key returns error.
behaviors.justification: This is function-level algorithm/lookup semantics within built-in library (MOCK-MISS: no for this behavior). No real infrastructure, no AI dependence, no multi-step agent flow. Rejected Integration+ because behavior text is limited to VLOOKUP function correctness, not sheet orchestration or persistence.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke VLOOKUP evaluator with controlled table arrays for hit/miss cases and assert exact returned value and not-found error code semantics.

behaviors.description: Nested IF conditions evaluate correctly (high/medium/low branching).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local expression evaluation and conditional branching semantics in one formula path (MOCK-MISS: no). No OS/network/subprocess, no AI/ML, no workflow chain. Rejected higher levels because behavior text specifies only nested IF logic outcome.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate nested IF expressions against boundary inputs (e.g., 10, 11, 20, 21) and assert exact branch outputs and comparison behavior.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE joins values with separator and skips empty cells (no blank-string insertions).
behaviors.justification: Function semantics are local string assembly rules; real failure does not require cross-module wiring (MOCK-MISS: no). No real infra, no AI output, no multi-step agent chain. Rejected Integration+ because behavior text is limited to CONCATENATE result formatting.
behaviors.test_description: Run CONCATENATE evaluation with mixed text/empty cells and separators, assert output tokens and separator placement omit empties correctly.

behaviors.description: Cross-sheet reference (`=Sheet2!A1+1`) resolves when referenced sheet exists.
behaviors.justification: Requires interaction between parser (sheet-qualified refs), sheet registry, and evaluator (MOCK-MISS: yes). Integration-brake: no mandatory OS/network/subprocess; no AI/ML; no multi-agent sequencing. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is in-memory cross-sheet resolution only.
behaviors.test_description: Create multi-sheet workbook fixture, evaluate formulas referencing existing and missing sheets, assert successful resolution for existing sheet and correct error for absent sheet.

behaviors.description: When a referenced cell changes, all dependents recalculate in correct topological order.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is dependency-tracking + recalculation scheduler handoff across modules (MOCK-MISS: yes). Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess needed; no AI output; no agentic chain. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text concerns internal recalculation ordering.
behaviors.test_description: Build dependency DAG with branching and depth, mutate a source cell, trigger recalc, and assert affected cells recompute once in dependency-safe order with expected final values.

behaviors.description: Undo after formula edit restores formula text and previously computed dependent values.
behaviors.justification: Needs coordination between edit history, formula store, and recomputation state restoration (MOCK-MISS: yes). Integration-brake: no required real OS/network/subprocess; no AI/ML; no multi-agent sequence. Rejected System because behavior text is about undo engine state, not disk/process boundaries; rejected Agentic/Workflow due no model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Perform formula edit in in-memory workbook with dependents, capture baseline, execute edit then undo, assert restored formula AST/text and all downstream cell values match baseline snapshot.

behaviors.description: Save workbook to disk and load back with identical formulas/values, including floating-point precision stability.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly crosses real filesystem boundary ("to disk and loading it back"); serialization/deserialization + file I/O interaction can hide failures under in-process fakes (REAL-INFRA: yes, MOCK-HIDE: yes). No AI dependence or agentic sequence. Rejected Integration because concrete behavior text requires real disk persistence fidelity, including binary-format and precision round-trip.
behaviors.test_description: Write workbook with representative formulas and high-precision floats to an actual temp file on disk, reload via normal loader, and assert byte-level/semantic round-trip invariants for formulas and numeric values within defined precision tolerance.
